title: Deliciously Smoky American Corn Skillet
description: A fermented and crumbly American dish featuring soured cream and chive dip, robata-grilled to perfection in a cast-iron skillet, suitable for a Buddhist temple gathering.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the soured cream, chives, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the corn kernels to the bowl and stir until they are well coated with the cream mixture.
Heat the olive oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the corn mixture to the skillet and cook for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated robata grill and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
If using cheese, sprinkle it on top of the corn mixture and cook for an additional 2 minutes, until melted and bubbly.
Remove the skillet from the grill and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the smoky American corn skillet hot, garnished with additional chives if desired.
